audience: Estate agents, property developers, landlords, architects and owners planning photography for sale, rental or project communications.
primaryDutchSearchIntent: Makelaars en eigenaars helpen een pand doelgericht voorbereiden en volledig, verzorgd en geloofwaardig laten fotograferen.

searchIntent.question: How do you prepare and photograph a property completely, professionally and honestly for sale, rental or project communications?
searchIntent.geographicContext: International English-speaking readers, with Belgian image-rights and copyright context and VisualVibe services based in Limburg, Belgium.

directAnswer: Good real estate photography helps prospective buyers or tenants understand a property's layout, connections and genuine finishes. Prepare every space, use a logical shot list, manage light and perspective carefully, check privacy and present the property attractively without making it appear larger, brighter or more luxurious than it is.
title: Real estate photography tips: an honest, complete property story
metaDescription: Learn which property photos every listing needs, how to prepare and present spaces honestly, and what to agree about timing, privacy and usage rights.
h1: Real estate photography tips: an honest, complete property story

internalLinks.anchor: local SEO for SMEs in Limburg

factsToPreserve: Professional photography does not guarantee a faster transaction, higher price or more enquiries.
Extreme wide angles and heavy editing can distort expectations.
Photography should not hide permanent defects, dimensions, surroundings or finishes.
Virtual staging must be labelled clearly and realistic images of the current state should remain available.
Consent to create an image and consent to publish it are assessed separately.
Usage, editing, transfer and reuse rights should be agreed with photographer, agent and client.
Image SEO and alt-text practices improve implementation but do not guarantee traffic or visibility.
Comparing sales outcomes requires caution because price, location, season and market demand also vary.
